transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Horizon Mortgage Servicing. My name is Sarah. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, I just received a letter saying my mortgage payment is increasing by almost two hundred dollars. It mentions something about escrow and I don't understand why.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand that can be frustrating. Let me take a look at your account and explain what changed. May I verify your name and the property zip code first?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure, it's John Miller and the property zip code is 1 1 7 9 0.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. I have your account here. I can see that we recently completed your annual escrow analysis.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay, but what exactly does that mean?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Each year we review the escrow account to make sure there is enough money to pay your property taxes and homeowners insurance when those bills come due.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: In your case, your county property taxes increased this year and your insurance premium also went up slightly. Because of that, the escrow account had a shortage.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So the higher payment is basically covering those increases?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's correct. Part of the increase covers the higher projected taxes and insurance, and part of it repays the escrow shortage from the past year.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Is there any way to avoid paying the shortage all at once?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes. You have two options. You can pay the shortage as a lump sum now, which would lower your monthly payment, or we can spread the shortage over the next twelve months.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I think spreading it out over the year would be easier for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's completely fine. The payment amount you saw in the letter already reflects the shortage being spread over twelve months.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Will it go back down next year?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: It may. If taxes and insurance remain stable and there is no shortage next year, your payment could decrease after the next escrow analysis.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can also email you the escrow analysis statement if you'd like to review the numbers in detail.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, that would be helpful.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. I'll send that to the email address we have on file. Is there anything else I can help you with today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that answers my question. Thanks for explaining it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're very welcome. Thank you for calling Horizon Mortgage Servicing and have a great day.
